req.title: Interest accrual (negative variant)
req.statement: A savings account shall accrue interest daily using the tier rate and credit interest monthly on the last business day. Negative paths (insufficient funds, unauthorised, exceeded limits) must be tested.

tests.name: naive_case
tests.executable:
# Pytest Tests for Savings Account Daily Accrual & Monthly Interest Crediting

```python
"""
Test Suite: Savings Account Interest Accrual & Crediting
Requirement: A savings account shall accrue interest daily using the tier rate
             and credit interest monthly on the last business day.
             Negative paths (insufficient funds, unauthorised, exceeded limits)
             must be tested.
"""

import pytest
from decimal import Decimal
from datetime import date, datetime
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, PropertyMock
from freezegun import freeze_time


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Domain / application imports (adjust to your actual module paths)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
from banking.accounts import SavingsAccount
from banking.interest import (
    InterestAccrualEngine,
    InterestCreditingService,
    TierRateSchedule,
)
from banking.calendar import BusinessCalendar
from banking.exceptions import (
    InsufficientFundsError,
    UnauthorisedAccessError,
    DepositLimitExceededError,
    WithdrawalLimitExceededError,
    AccountFrozenError,
    NegativeBalanceError,
    InvalidTierRateError,
)


# ===========================================================================
# Fixtures
# ===========================================================================

@pytest.fixture
def tier_rate_schedule():
    """Standard tiered interest rate schedule."""
    return TierRateSchedule(
        tiers=[
            {"min": Decimal("0.00"),       "max": Decimal("999.99"),    "annual_rate": Decimal("0.01")},   # 1 %
            {"min": Decimal("1000.00"),    "max": Decimal("9999.99"),   "annual_rate": Decimal("0.02")},   # 2 %
            {"min": Decimal("10000.00"),   "max": Decimal("49999.99"),  "annual_rate": Decimal("0.03")},   # 3 %
            {"min": Decimal("50000.00"),   "max": Decimal("99999999"),  "annual_rate": Decimal("0.04")},   # 4 %
        ]
    )


@pytest.fixture
def business_calendar():
    """Business calendar with standard UK bank holidays."""
    cal = BusinessCalendar(country="GB")
    return cal


@pytest.fixture
def savings_account(tier_rate_schedule):
    """Standard savings account with a mid-tier opening balance."""
    return SavingsAccount(
        account_id="SA-001",
        owner_id="CUST-001",
        balance=Decimal("5000.00"),
        tier_rate_schedule=tier_rate_schedule,
        daily_deposit_limit=Decimal("10000.00"),
        daily_withdrawal_limit=Decimal("2000.00"),
    )


@pytest.fixture
def zero_balance_account(tier_rate_schedule):
    """Savings account with zero balance."""
    return SavingsAccount(
        account_id="SA-002",
        owner_id="CUST-002",
        balance=Decimal("0.00"),
        tier_rate_schedule=tier_rate_schedule,
        daily_deposit_limit=Decimal("10000.00"),
        daily_withdrawal_limit=Decimal("2000.00"),
    )


@pytest.fixture
def high_balance_account(tier_rate_schedule):
    """Savings account qualifying for the highest tier."""
    return SavingsAccount(
        account_id="SA-003",
        owner_id="CUST-003",
        balance=Decimal("75000.00"),
        tier_rate_schedule=tier_rate_schedule,
        daily_deposit_limit=Decimal("50000.00"),
        daily_withdrawal_limit=Decimal("10000.00"),
    )


@pytest.fixture
def frozen_account(tier_rate_schedule):
    """Savings account that has been frozen."""
    account = SavingsAccount(
        account_id="SA-004",
        owner_id="CUST-004",
        balance=Decimal("3000.00"),
        tier_rate_schedule=tier_rate_schedule,
        daily_deposit_limit=Decimal("10000.00"),
        daily_withdrawal_limit=Decimal("2000.00"),
    )
    account.freeze()
    return account


@pytest.fixture
def accrual_engine(savings_account, business_calendar):
    return InterestAccrualEngine(
        account=savings_account,
        calendar=business_calendar,
    )


@pytest.fixture
def crediting_service(savings_account, business_calendar):
    return InterestCreditingService(
        account=savings_account,
        calendar=business_calendar,
    )


# ===========================================================================
# 1. Tier Rate Assignment
# ===========================================================================

class TestTierRateAssignment:
    """Verify the correct annual rate is selected for each balance tier."""

    @pytest.mark.parametrize(
        "balance, expected_rate",
        [
            (Decimal("0.00"),       Decimal("0.01")),   # floor of tier 1
            (Decimal("500.00"),     Decimal("0.01")),   # mid tier 1
            (Decimal("999.99"),     Decimal("0.01")),   # ceiling of tier 1
            (Decimal("1000.00"),    Decimal("0.02")),   # floor of tier 2
            (Decimal("5000.00"),    Decimal("0.02")),   # mid tier 2
            (Decimal("9999.99"),    Decimal("0.02")),   # ceiling of tier 2
            (Decimal("10000.00"),   Decimal("0.03")),   # floor of tier 3
            (Decimal("30000.00"),   Decimal("0.03")),   # mid tier 3
            (Decimal("49999.99"),   Decimal("0.03")),   # ceiling of tier 3
            (Decimal("50000.00"),   Decimal("0.04")),   # floor of tier 4
            (Decimal("75000.00"),   Decimal("0.04")),   # mid tier 4
            (Decimal("99999999"),   Decimal("0.04")),   # very large balance
        ],
    )
    def test_correct_rate_returned_for_balance(
        self, tier_rate_schedule, balance, expected_rate
    ):
        rate = tier_rate_schedule.get_rate(balance)
        assert rate == expected_rate, (
            f"Expected {expected_rate} for balance {balance}, got {rate}"
        )

    def test_negative_balance_raises_error(self, tier_rate_schedule):
        with pytest.raises(NegativeBalanceError):
            tier_rate_schedule.get_rate(Decimal("-100.00"))

    def test_invalid_tier_schedule_raises_error(self):
        with pytest.raises(InvalidTierRateError):
            TierRateSchedule(tiers=[])  # empty schedule is invalid

    def test_overlapping_tiers_raise_error(self):
        with pytest.raises(InvalidTierRateError):
            TierRateSchedule(
                tiers=[
                    {"min": Decimal("0"),    "max": Decimal("1000"), "annual_rate": Decimal("0.01")},
                    {"min": Decimal("500"),  "max": Decimal("2000"), "annual_rate": Decimal("0.02")},  # overlaps
                ]
            )


# ===========================================================================
# 2. Daily Interest Accrual – Happy Paths
# ===========================================================================

class TestDailyInterestAccrual:
    """Daily accrual must use balance × (annual_rate / 365)."""

    def test_single_day_accrual_mid_tier(self, savings_account, accrual_engine):
        """5 000 @ 2 % / 365 ≈ 0.273972..."""
        with freeze_time("2024-03-15"):
            accrual = accrual_engine.accrue_for_day(date(2024, 3, 15))

        expected = (Decimal("5000.00") * Decimal("0.02") / Decimal("365")).quantize(
            Decimal("0.000001")
        )
        assert accrual == pytest.approx(float(expected), rel=1e-6)

    def test_single_day_accrual_high_tier(self, high_balance_account, business_calendar):
        """75 000 @ 4 % / 365."""
        engine = InterestAccrualEngine(
            account=high_balance_account, calendar=business_calendar
        )
        with freeze_time("2024-03-15"):
            accrual = engine.accrue_for_day(date(2024, 3, 15))

        expected = (Decimal("75000.00") * Decimal("0.04") / Decimal("365")).quantize(
            Decimal("0.000001")
        )
        assert accrual == pytest.approx(float(expected), rel=1e-6)

    def test_accrual_zero_balance(self, zero_balance_account, business_calendar):
        """Zero balance should produce zero accrual."""
        engine = InterestAccrualEngine(
            account=zero_balance_account, calendar=business_calendar
        )
        accrual = engine.accrue_for_day(date(2024, 3, 15))
        assert accrual == Decimal("0.00")

    def test_accrual_accumulates_over_month(self, savings_account, accrual_engine):
        """Accumulated accrual over 31 days must equal sum of daily accruals."""
        total = Decimal("0.00")
        for day in range(1, 32):   # March 2024 has 31 days
            total += accrual_engine.accrue_for_day(date(2024, 3, day))

        running_total = savings_account.accrued_interest
        assert running_total == pytest.approx(float(total), rel=1e-6)

    def test_accrual_uses_end_of_day_balance(
        self, savings_account, accrual_engine
    ):
        """If a deposit is made mid-day, accrual must use the closing balance."""
        savings_account.deposit(Decimal("2000.00"), actor_id="CUST-001")
        # Balance is now 7 000 – still tier 2 @ 2 %
        accrual = accrual_engine.accrue_for_day(date(2024, 3, 15))
        expected = (Decimal("7000.00") * Decimal("0.02") / Decimal("365")).quantize(
            Decimal("0.000001")
        )
        assert accrual == pytest.approx(float(expected), rel=1e-6)

    def test_accrual_stored_separately_from_principal(
        self, savings_account, accrual_engine
    ):
        """Accrued interest must NOT yet be added to the principal balance."""
        before_balance = savings_account.balance
        accrual_engine.accrue_for_day(date(2024, 3, 15))
        assert savings_account.balance == before_balance

    def test_accrual_tier_boundary_crossed_during_month(
        self, savings_account, accrual_engine, business_calendar
    ):
        """
        If a deposit pushes balance into a new tier, subsequent daily
        accruals must use the new rate.
        """
        # Day 1 – still 5 000 (tier 2 @ 2 %)
        accrual_day1 = accrual_engine.accrue_for_day(date(2024, 3, 1))
        expected_day1 = (Decimal("5000.00") * Decimal("0.02") / Decimal("365"))
        assert accrual_day1 == pytest.approx(float(expected_day1), rel=1e-6)

        # Deposit pushes balance to 12 000 (tier 3 @ 3 %)
        savings_account.deposit(Decimal("7000.00"), actor_id="CUST-001")

        # Day 2 – new tier applies
        accrual_day2 = accrual_engine.accrue_for_day(date(2024, 3, 2))
        expected_day2 = (Decimal("12000.00") * Decimal("0.03") / Decimal("365"))
        assert accrual_day2 == pytest.approx(float(expected_day2), rel=1e-6)

    @pytest.mark.parametrize("year", [2020, 2024])
    def test_accrual_uses_366_days_in_leap_year(
        self, savings_account, business_calendar, year
    ):
        """Leap year must divide by 366."""
        engine = InterestAccrualEngine(
            account=savings_account, calendar=business_calendar
        )
        accrual = engine.accrue_for_day(date(year, 2, 29))  # leap day
        expected = (Decimal("5000.00") * Decimal("0.02") / Decimal("366")).quantize(
            Decimal("0.000001")
        )
        assert accrual == pytest.approx(float(expected), rel=1e-6)

    def test_accrual_on_weekend_still_runs(self, savings_account, accrual_engine):
        """Interest accrues every calendar day including weekends."""
        saturday = date(2024, 3, 16)  # known Saturday
        accrual = accrual_engine.accrue_for_day(saturday)
        assert accrual > Decimal("0.00")

    def test_accrual_on_bank_holiday_still_runs(
        self, savings_account, business_calendar
    ):
        """Interest accrues on public holidays."""
        engine = InterestAccrualEngine(
            account=savings_account, calendar=business_calendar
        )
        # Christmas Day 2024
        accrual = engine.accrue_for_day(date(2024, 12, 25))
        assert accrual > Decimal("0.00")


# ===========================================================================
# 3. Monthly Interest Crediting – Happy Paths
# ===========================================================================

class TestMonthlyInterestCrediting:
    """Accrued interest must be credited on the last business day of each month."""

    def test_credit_on_last_business_day_of_month(
        self, crediting_service, savings_account
    ):
        """
        March 2024: last calendar day is 31st (Sunday).
        Last business day is Friday 29th.
        """
        last_biz_day = crediting_service.last_business_day(year=2024, month=3)
        assert last_biz_day == date(2024, 3, 29)

    def test_interest_credited_increases_balance(
        self, savings_account, accrual_engine, crediting_service
    ):
        """After crediting, principal balance must increase by accrued amount."""
        # Accrue for the entire month of March 2024
        for day in range(1, 32):
            accrual_engine.accrue_for_day(date(2024, 3, day))

        accrued = savings_account.accrued_interest
        balance_before = savings_account.balance

        with freeze_time("2024-03-29"):  # last business day
            crediting_service.credit_monthly_interest(year=2024, month=3)

        assert savings_account.balance == balance_before + accrued

    def test_accrued_interest_reset_after_crediting(
        self, savings_account, accrual_engine, crediting_service
    ):
        """Pending accrued interest ledger must be zero after crediting."""
        for day in range(1, 32):
            accrual_engine.accrue_for_day(date(2024, 3, day))

        with freeze_time("2024-03-29"):
            crediting_service.credit_monthly_interest(year=2024, month=3)

        assert savings_account.accrued_interest == Decimal("0.00")

    def test_crediting_creates_transaction_record(
        self, savings_account, accrual_engine, crediting_service
    ):
        """A credit transaction must appear in the account ledger."""
        for day in range(1, 32):
            accrual_engine.accrue_for_day(date(2024, 3, day))

        with freeze_time("2024-03-29"):
            crediting_service.credit_monthly_interest(year=2024, month=3)

        interest_txns = [
            t for t in savings_account.transactions
            if t.transaction_type == "INTEREST_CREDIT"
        ]
        assert len(interest_txns) == 1
        assert interest_txns[0].value_date == date(2024, 3, 29)

    def test_last_business_day_when_month_ends_on_friday(
        self, crediting_service
    ):
        """May 2024 ends on Friday the 31st – last biz day is 31st."""
        last_biz_day = crediting_service.last_business_day(year=2024, month=5)
        assert last_biz_day == date(2024, 5, 31)

    def test_last_business_day_when_month_end_is_bank_holiday(
        self, crediting_service
    ):
        """
        When the last calendar day is a bank holiday, roll back to the
        previous business day.
        """
        # Simulate December 2024: 31st is Tuesday but we'll mock it as holiday
        with patch.object(
            BusinessCalendar, "is_business_day", side_effect=lambda d: d != date(2024, 12, 31)
        ):
            last_biz_day = crediting_service.last_business_day(year=2024, month=12)
        assert last_biz_day == date(2024, 12, 30)

    def test_no_crediting_before_last_business_day(
        self, savings_account, accrual_engine, crediting_service
    ):
        """Crediting attempted before the last business day must be a no-op."""
        for day in range(1, 16):
            accrual_engine.accrue_for_day(date(2024, 3, day))

        accrued_mid_month = savings_account.accrued_interest
        balance_before = savings_account.balance

        with freeze_time("2024-03-15"):   # not the last business day
            crediting_service.credit_monthly_interest(year=2024, month=3)

        assert savings_account.balance == balance_before
        assert savings_account.accrued_interest == accrued_mid_month

    def test_crediting_fires_exactly_once_per_month(
        self, savings_account, accrual_engine, crediting_service
    ):
        """Double-triggering the credit job on the same day must credit once."""
        for day in range(1, 32):
            accrual_engine.accrue_for_day(date(2024, 3, day))

        with freeze_time("2024-03-29"):
            crediting_service.credit_monthly_interest(year=2024, month=3)
            crediting_service.credit_monthly_interest(year=2024, month=3)  # duplicate

        interest_txns = [
            t for t in savings_account.transactions
            if t.transaction_type == "INTEREST_CREDIT"
        ]
        assert len(interest_txns) == 1

    def test_twelve_month_crediting_cycle(
        self, savings_account, accrual_engine, crediting_service
    ):
        """Run full year; verify 12 crediting events and growing balance."""
        import calendar as cal_module

        total_credited = Decimal("0.00")
        for month in range(1, 13):
            _, days_in_month = cal_module.monthrange(2024, month)
            for day in range(1, days_in_month + 1):
                accrual_engine.accrue_for_day(date(2024, month, day))

            accrued = savings_account.accrued_interest
            last_biz = crediting_service.last_business_day(2024, month)
            with freeze_time(last_biz):
                crediting_service.credit_monthly_interest(year=2024, month=month)
            total_credited += accrued

        interest_txns = [
            t for t in savings_account.transactions
            if t.transaction_type == "INTEREST_CREDIT"
        ]
        assert len(interest_txns) == 12
        assert savings_account.balance > Decimal("5000.00")


# ===========================================================================
# 4. Negative Path – Insufficient Funds
# ===========================================================================

class TestInsufficientFunds:
    """Withdrawals that would result in a negative balance must be rejected."""

    def test_withdrawal_exceeding_balance_raises(self, savings_account):
        with pytest.raises(InsufficientFundsError) as exc_info:
            savings_account.withdraw(Decimal("6000.00"), actor_id="CUST-001")
        assert "insufficient" in str(exc_info.value).lower()

    def test_withdrawal_exactly_at_balance_succeeds(self, savings_account):
        """Withdrawing the exact balance should be permitted."""
        savings_account.withdraw(Decimal("5000.00"), actor_id="CUST-001")
        assert savings_account.balance == Decimal("0.00")

    def test_withdrawal_of_zero_raises(self, savings_account):
        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="amount must be positive"):
            savings_account.withdraw(Decimal("0.00"), actor_id="CUST-001")

    def test_withdrawal_of_negative_amount_raises(self, savings_account):
        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="amount must be positive"):
            savings_account.withdraw(Decimal("-100.00"), actor_id="CUST-001")

    def test_balance_unchanged_after_failed_withdrawal(self, savings_account):
        initial_balance = savings_account.balance
        with pytest.raises(InsufficientFundsError):
            savings_account.withdraw(Decimal("9999.00"), actor_id="CUST-001")
        assert savings_account.balance == initial_balance

    def test_no_transaction_record_on_failed_withdrawal(self, savings_account):
        txn_count_before = len(savings_account.transactions)
        with pytest.raises(InsufficientFundsError):
            savings_account.withdraw(Decimal("9999.00"), actor_id="CUST-001")
        assert len(savings_account.transactions) == txn_count_before

    def test_accrued_interest_not_available_for_withdrawal(
        self, savings_account, accrual_engine
    ):
        """Accrued-but-not-yet-credited interest is not spendable principal."""
        for day in range(1, 16):
            accrual_engine.accrue_for_day(date(2024, 3, day))

        # Try to withdraw balance + some accrued interest
        with pytest.raises(InsufficientFundsError):
            savings_account.withdraw(Decimal("5001.00"), actor_id="CUST-001")


# ===========================================================================
# 5. Negative Path – Unauthorised Access
# ===========================================================================

class TestUnauthorisedAccess:
    """Operations by non-owners or unauthenticated callers must be rejected."""

    def test_withdrawal_by_wrong_customer_raises(self, savings_account):
        with pytest.raises(UnauthorisedAccessError):
            savings_account.withdraw(Decimal("100.00"), actor_id="CUST-EVIL")

    def test_deposit_by_wrong_customer_raises(self, savings_account):
        with pytest.raises(UnauthorisedAccessError):
            savings_account.deposit(Decimal("100.00"), actor_id="CUST-EVIL")

    def test_view_balance_by_wrong_customer_raises(self, savings_account):
        with pytest.raises(UnauthorisedAccessError):
            savings_account.get_balance(actor_id="CUST-EVIL")

    def test_interest_crediting_by_non_system_actor_raises(
        self, crediting_service
    ):
        """Only the system / batch job role may trigger interest crediting."""
        with pytest.raises(UnauthorisedAccessError):
            crediting_service.credit_monthly_interest(
                year=2024, month=3, actor_id="CUST-001"
            )

    def test_operation_with_none_actor_raises(self, savings_account):
        with pytest.raises(UnauthorisedAccessError):
            savings_account.withdraw(Decimal("100.00"), actor_id=None)

    def test_operation_with_empty_actor_raises(self, savings_account):
        with pytest.raises(UnauthorisedAccessError):
            savings_account.withdraw(Decimal("100.00"), actor_id="")

    def test_frozen_account_blocks_all_operations(self, frozen_account):
        with pytest.raises(AccountFrozenError):
            frozen_account.withdraw(Decimal("100.00"), actor_id="CUST-004")

        with pytest.raises(AccountFrozenError):
            frozen_account.deposit(Decimal("100.00"), actor_id="CUST-004")

    def test_frozen_account_still_accrues_interest(
        self, frozen_account, business_calendar
    ):
        """Interest accrual is a system function and must continue on frozen accounts."""
        engine = InterestAccrualEngine(
            account=frozen_account, calendar=business_calendar
        )
        accrual = engine.accrue_for_day(date(2024, 3, 15))
        assert accrual > Decimal("0.00")


# ===========================================================================
# 6. Negative Path – Exceeded Limits
# ===========================================================================

class TestExceededLimits:
    """Daily deposit and withdrawal caps must be enforced."""

    def test_single_deposit_exceeding_daily_limit_raises(self, savings_account):
        with pytest.raises(DepositLimitExceededError) as exc_info:
            savings_account.deposit(Decimal("15000.00"), actor_id="CUST-001")
        assert "limit" in str(exc_info.value).lower()

    def test_cumulative_deposits_exceeding_daily_limit_raises(
        self, savings_account
    ):
        """Multiple deposits in one day must not collectively exceed the cap."""
        savings_account.deposit(Decimal("7000.00"), actor_id="CUST-001")  # 7 000 of 10 000
        with pytest.raises(DepositLimitExceededError):
            savings_account.deposit(Decimal("4000.00"), actor_id="CUST-001")  # would be 11 000

    def test_deposit_exactly_at_limit_succeeds(self, savings_account):
        savings_account.deposit(Decimal("10000.00"), actor_id="CUST-001")
        assert savings_account.balance == Decimal("15000.00")

    def test_single_withdrawal_exceeding_daily_limit_raises(
        self, savings_account
    ):
        with pytest.raises(WithdrawalLimitExceededError):
            savings_account.withdraw(Decimal("3000.00"), actor_id="CUST-001")

    def test_cumulative_withdrawals_exceeding_daily_limit_raises(
        self, savings_account
    ):
        savings_account.withdraw(Decimal("1500.00"), actor_id="CUST-001")  # 1 500 of 2 000
        with pytest.raises(WithdrawalLimitExceededError):
            savings_account.withdraw(Decimal("1000.00"), actor_id="CUST-001")  # would be 2 500

    def test_withdrawal_exactly_at_daily_limit_succeeds(self, savings_account):
        savings_account.withdraw(Decimal("2000.00"), actor_id="CUST-001")
        assert savings_account.balance == Decimal("3000.00")

    def test_daily_limits_reset_at_midnight(self, savings_account):
        """Limits from day N must not carry over to day N+1."""
        with freeze_time("2024-03-15"):
            savings_account.withdraw(Decimal("2000.00"), actor_id="CUST-001")

        with freeze_time("2024-03-16"):  # new day – limit resets
            savings_account.withdraw(Decimal("2000.00"), actor_id="CUST-001")

        assert savings_account.balance == Decimal("1000.00")

    def test_balance_unchanged_after_deposit_limit_breach(
        self, savings_account
    ):
        initial_balance = savings_account.balance
        with pytest.raises(DepositLimitExceededError):
            savings_account.deposit(Decimal("15000.00"), actor_id="CUST-001")
        assert savings_account.balance == initial_balance

    def test_balance_unchanged_after_withdrawal_limit_breach(
        self, savings_account
    ):
        initial_balance = savings_account.balance
        with pytest.raises(WithdrawalLimitExceededError):
            savings_account.withdraw(Decimal("3000.00"), actor_id="CUST-001")
        assert savings_account.balance == initial_balance

    def test_deposit_of_zero_raises_value_error(self, savings_account):
        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="amount must be positive"):
            savings_account.deposit(Decimal("0.00"), actor_id="CUST-001")


# ===========================================================================
# 7. Integration – End-to-End Accrual & Crediting Cycle
# ===========================================================================

class TestEndToEndAccrualCreditingCycle:
    """
    Simulate a realistic month-end scenario covering accrual, limit events,
    and final crediting on the last business day.
    """

    def test_full_march_2024_cycle(
        self, savings_account, accrual_engine, crediting_service
    ):
        """
        March 2024:
        - 31 calendar days
        - Last business day = Friday 29th
        - Expected total accrual = sum(5000 × 0.02 / 365) for each day
        """
        import calendar as cal_module

        _, days = cal_module.monthrange(2024, 3)
        expected_total = sum(
            Decimal("5000.00") * Decimal("0.02") / Decimal("365")
            for _ in range(days)
        )

        for day in range(1, days + 1):
            accrual_engine.accrue_for_day(date(2024, 3, day))

        assert savings_account.accrued_interest == pytest.approx(
            float(expected_total), rel=1e-6
        )

        balance_before = savings_account.balance
        with freeze_time("2024-03-29"):
            crediting_service.credit_monthly_interest(year=2024, month=3)

        assert savings_account.balance > balance_before
        assert savings_account.accrued_interest == Decimal("0.00")
